patientStory:
[REF2]61-year-old[/REF2] [REF3]female presenting for[/REF3] follow-up visit. She carries diagnoses of [REF5]idiopathic gout of the right wrist (active since 10/21/2025)[/REF5], [REF6]right wrist pain[/REF6], and [REF7]contusion of right upper arm[/REF7]. [REF33]Right wrist pain was documented on 03/19/2024,[/REF33] [REF34]on 10/06/2025,[/REF34] [REF35]on 10/21/2025,[/REF35] and [REF36]on 11/13/2025,[/REF36] [REF37]showing a recurrent pattern from 03/19/2024 to 11/13/2025[/REF37]. [REF23]She reported similar symptoms in March 2023 and July 2023 that were treated with colchicine.[/REF23] [REF10]Colchicine 0.6 mg oral tablet[/REF10] was recently prescribed with [REF9]dosage adjustment required due to drug interaction with carvedilol[/REF9] [REF12]12.5 mg twice daily[/REF12].

[REF22]On 10/21/2025, the patient presented with right wrist pain without history of injury[/REF22] and [REF25]examination showed swelling of the dorsal aspect of right wrist without erythema[/REF25]. [REF24]She stated she did not have any colchicine at home.[/REF24] [REF26]Radiographs from 10/06/2024 were reviewed, which[/REF26] [REF27]demonstrated a cyst in the ulnar aspect of lunate with positive ulnar variance[/REF27] and [REF28]calcification in soft tissues dorsal to the lunate[/REF28]. [REF16]Uric acid was elevated at 9.6 mg/dL.[/REF16] [REF29]The impression was possible gout flare of the right wrist,[/REF29] and [REF30]the patient was also noted to have cysts in the ulnar aspect of lunate possibly secondary to ulnar abutment syndrome[/REF30]; [REF31]a rheumatoid panel and[/REF31] [REF32]MRI of the right wrist were recommended[/REF32].

raw:
PATIENT STORY ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

[REF2]61-year-old[/REF2] [REF3]female presenting for[/REF3] follow-up visit. She carries diagnoses of [REF5]idiopathic gout of the right wrist (active since 10/21/2025)[/REF5], [REF6]right wrist pain[/REF6], and [REF7]contusion of right upper arm[/REF7]. [REF33]Right wrist pain was documented on 03/19/2024,[/REF33] [REF34]on 10/06/2025,[/REF34] [REF35]on 10/21/2025,[/REF35] and [REF36]on 11/13/2025,[/REF36] [REF37]showing a recurrent pattern from 03/19/2024 to 11/13/2025[/REF37]. [REF23]She reported similar symptoms in March 2023 and July 2023 that were treated with colchicine.[/REF23] [REF10]Colchicine 0.6 mg oral tablet[/REF10] was recently prescribed with [REF9]dosage adjustment required due to drug interaction with carvedilol[/REF9] [REF12]12.5 mg twice daily[/REF12].

[REF22]On 10/21/2025, the patient presented with right wrist pain without history of injury[/REF22] and [REF25]examination showed swelling of the dorsal aspect of right wrist without erythema[/REF25]. [REF24]She stated she did not have any colchicine at home.[/REF24] [REF26]Radiographs from 10/06/2024 were reviewed, which[/REF26] [REF27]demonstrated a cyst in the ulnar aspect of lunate with positive ulnar variance[/REF27] and [REF28]calcification in soft tissues dorsal to the lunate[/REF28]. [REF16]Uric acid was elevated at 9.6 mg/dL.[/REF16] [REF29]The impression was possible gout flare of the right wrist,[/REF29] and [REF30]the patient was also noted to have cysts in the ulnar aspect of lunate possibly secondary to ulnar abutment syndrome[/REF30]; [REF31]a rheumatoid panel and[/REF31] [REF32]MRI of the right wrist were recommended[/REF32].

SINCE LAST VISIT ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Laboratory Results (10/22/2025):
Rheumatoid panel testing completed.
· [REF19]Rheumatoid factor 10 IU/mL (normal)[/REF19]

Laboratory Results (10/24/2025):
Additional autoimmune workup completed.
· [REF20]ANA by IFA negative (normal)[/REF20]
· [REF21]CCP antibodies IgG/IgA 6 U (normal)[/REF21]

VISIT PRIORITIES ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

TODAY'S VISIT — MUST ADDRESS

[REF16]Elevated Uric Acid[/REF16]
9.6 mg/dL on 10/21/2025 in setting of suspected gout flare

[REF17]Elevated Sedimentation Rate[/REF17]
30 mm/h on 10/21/2025

[REF9]Colchicine-Carvedilol Drug Interaction[/REF9]
Dosage adjustment required; confirm current regimen tolerability

[REF30]Lunate Cysts with Possible Ulnar Abutment Syndrome[/REF30]
Noted on 10/21/2025; may contribute to recurrent wrist symptoms

OUTSTANDING — CONFIRM STATUS

[REF32]MRI Right Wrist[/REF32]
Recommended 10/21/2025; completion status unknown
